calendar.matters:
PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-09-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-20-303983 Estate of Lois May Gintjee and
PES-20-303984 Estate of Thomas Gintjee.

Appearances: Timothy Gavin appeared by zoom for Benjamin Gintjee, Thomas Gintjee, Kathy Thorpe and Michael Gintjee, and for and with Anthony Gintjee (by zoom).

Mr. Gavin stated that he was not aware that Brian had a trust, and that Anthony and Michael are the sole Beneficiaries of that trust. The Court stated that it thinks they have a clear path on Benjamin's petition, and a clear path on Thomas and Kathy's petition, and that it can revise the order to indicate that Michael and Anthony take as Trustees of Brian's trust. Mr. Gavin stated that they can work with that. The Petitions to Determine Succession to Real Property (2) are under submission for review and modification of the orders with the intent to grant the petitions.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Nov-16-2020 Continued To Dec-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PES-20-303983 Estate of Lois May Gintjee and PES-20-303984 Estate of Thomas Gintjee.

Appearances: Timothy Gavin appeared by zoom for Petitioners.

The Court stated that it thinks some of these petitions are on solid ground (Benjamin's petition to succeed to Lois), that there are too many layers in between to grant Michael and Anthony's petition to succeed to Lois, asked if Brian died intestate, or testate, asked how the interests were passed to Michael and Anthony, asked if new deeds were recorded after Martha's death, etc. Mr. Gavin provided history, stated that the Marin Court approved that William's share go directly to the two Grandsons, that some of the issues were addressed in his supplemental declaration that was filed on November 9th, that Martha left the property as tenants in common to the four children, that there was one estate plan for Irene and that he does not have the Marin County documents in hand. The Court stated that the documents that were provided in Marin may illuminate this. Mr. Gavin expressed concern about the buyers stating that they have called twice and are getting nervous about the sale, and stated that if there are any creditors, the petitioners are liable for those debts. The Court stated that there is a question of testacy, or intestacy. The Petitions to Determine Succession to Real Property are continued to December 7, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Ex Parte Petition For Order Shortening Time For Hearing On Petition To Determine Succession To Real Property
